SEARCH
Package bupt.ta.llm

Class DeepSeekClient

java.lang.Object
bupt.ta.llm.DeepSeekClient
public final class DeepSeekClient extends Object
OpenAI-compatible chat API client (POST /v1/chat/completions).

Configuration resolution (highest priority first):

  1. Admin-managed JSON (data/ai-api-settings.json) when enabled and a key is set.
  2. Environment variables / JVM properties (TA_AI_*, LLM_*, MIMO_*, OPENAI_*, DEEPSEEK_*) via fromRuntimeSettings(AiApiSettings) — used when admin settings are empty so local ai.env + run-with-ai.ps1 work without re-entering the key in Admin UI.
  3. No-arg DeepSeekClient() for tests and CLI (env only).
Never commit API keys; use env vars, CI secrets, or the admin settings file.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    DeepSeekClient()
    Environment-driven construction.
    DeepSeekClient(boolean enabled, String apiKey, String baseUrl, String model)
    Full constructor allowing an explicit enabled flag, used by fromAdminSettings(AiApiSettings) so the admin toggle bypasses env-var checks.
    DeepSeekClient(String apiKey, String baseUrl, String model)
    Explicit configuration; the client is treated as enabled regardless of environment.

  • Constructor Details

    • DeepSeekClient

      public DeepSeekClient()
      Environment-driven construction. Reads TA_AI_*, generic LLM_*, provider-specific env vars, and matching JVM system properties. Used by integration tests and stand-alone CLIs.

    • DeepSeekClient

      public DeepSeekClient(String apiKey, String baseUrl, String model)
      Explicit configuration; the client is treated as enabled regardless of environment.

    • DeepSeekClient

      public DeepSeekClient(boolean enabled, String apiKey, String baseUrl, String model)
      Full constructor allowing an explicit enabled flag, used by fromAdminSettings(AiApiSettings) so the admin toggle bypasses env-var checks.

  • Method Details

    • fromAdminSettings

      public static DeepSeekClient fromAdminSettings(AiApiSettings settings)
      Build a client from admin JSON only (no env fallback). Used by the admin settings form when displaying what is stored on disk.

    • fromRuntimeSettings

      public static DeepSeekClient fromRuntimeSettings(AiApiSettings stored)
      Resolve admin settings, falling back to environment variables when admin has no API key. This is the entry point for all servlet LLM calls.

    • isRuntimeConfigured

      public static boolean isRuntimeConfigured(AiApiSettings stored)
      Returns:
      true when fromRuntimeSettings(AiApiSettings) would be able to call the API

    • isConfigured

      public boolean isConfigured()
      Returns:
      true when enabled and a non-blank API key is configured

    • chat

      public String chat(String systemPrompt, String userMessage) throws IOException
      Single user message with optional system prompt.
      Parameters:
      systemPrompt - optional system role content (may be null)
      userMessage - required user message
      Returns:
      assistant reply text
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if not configured
      IOException - on HTTP or API errors

    • chatCompletions

      public String chatCompletions(com.google.gson.JsonArray messages) throws IOException
      Sends a chat completion request with full control over the messages array.
      Parameters:
      messages - OpenAI-format message array (roles and content)
      Returns:
      assistant reply text from the first choice
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if not configured
      IOException - on HTTP or API errors

    • chatStream

      public String chatStream(String systemPrompt, String userMessage, Consumer<String> onChunk) throws IOException
      Streaming counterpart of chat(String, String). Pushes each non-empty content delta from the OpenAI-compatible SSE response to onChunk. Returns the concatenation of all delivered chunks so callers can also persist or log the full text. The consumer is not called for the final [DONE] sentinel.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if not configured
      IOException - HTTP, parsing, or upstream API errors
